audience: Business stakeholders, product owners, CRO consultants, UX researchers, QA, delivery managers, non-technical leadership

layers.name: Audit Contract
layers.summary: What we agree to audit and why.

layers.name: Orchestration
layers.summary: The coordinator that runs the whole audit.

layers.name: Browser Perception
layers.summary: How the system actually sees a page.

layers.name: Dynamic State Coverage
layers.summary: Hidden views of each page (filters, modals, scroll).

layers.name: AI Analysis
layers.summary: AI applies expert rules to each page.

layers.name: Storage & Observability
layers.summary: Where everything is saved and tracked.

layers.name: Learning Loop (v1.1+)
layers.summary: Audits make the heuristic library smarter over time.

layers.name: Roadmap & Future
layers.summary: How the system evolves after MVP.

nodes.label: Consultant Trigger
nodes.purpose: The friendly front door for an audit.
what_happens: A consultant types the audit command or clicks Start in the dashboard.
The system collects the URL list and business type.
A new audit run is queued.
nodes.why_matters: One-click start. No config drama for the consultant.
nodes.example: A consultant runs an audit on https://example-shop.com using the 'ecommerce' template.

nodes.label: URL Discovery
nodes.purpose: Finds the right pages on the website to audit.
what_happens: Reads the sitemap or crawls the site.
Picks representative pages (home, category, product, cart).
Caps the page count to fit budget.
outputs: Curated URL list with page-type tags
nodes.why_matters: Removes the manual work of choosing which pages to audit.
nodes.example: Given example-shop.com, picks home + 2 categories + 2 product pages + cart + checkout.

nodes.label: Audit Template
nodes.purpose: Pre-built audit recipes by business type.
what_happens: Maps a business type to a default persona, page mix, and heuristic group.
Provides sensible defaults so audits don't need hand-tuning.
nodes.why_matters: Consultants get a smart starting point every time.
nodes.example: Choosing 'ecommerce' loads the 'shopper' persona and the e-com heuristic pack.

nodes.label: Client Profile
nodes.purpose: Identifies who the audit is for.
what_happens: Captures client name, brand context, sensitive constraints.
nodes.why_matters: Per-client context shapes findings and allows billing.
nodes.example: Audit tagged to client 'Acme Corp', region IN.

nodes.label: Business Type & Persona
nodes.purpose: Tells the system who the audit is performed for (the buyer).
what_happens: Sets a target persona used by AI prompts.
Adjusts heuristic emphasis to that persona.
nodes.why_matters: Findings reflect a real buyer, not a generic visitor.
nodes.example: 'Ecommerce → time-pressed mobile shopper'.

nodes.label: URL List & Page Types
nodes.purpose: Locks in which pages will be audited and their roles.
what_happens: Tags each URL as home / category / product / cart / etc.
nodes.why_matters: Page-type drives which heuristics apply.
nodes.example: Tag /products/shoe-1 as 'product detail page'.

nodes.label: Success Criteria
nodes.purpose: Defines what 'done' looks like for this audit.
what_happens: Sets budget, page count, severity thresholds.
nodes.why_matters: Predictable scope and predictable cost.
nodes.example: Cap = $15 per audit, top 10 findings only.

nodes.label: Audit Planner
nodes.purpose: Turns a request into an executable audit plan.
what_happens: Builds the page list and the order of work.
Allocates budget per page.
nodes.why_matters: Every audit follows the same disciplined plan.
nodes.example: Plan: 6 pages × 3 viewports, $2.50 budget per page.

nodes.label: Page Queue Manager
nodes.purpose: Schedules pages one by one so the system stays sane.
what_happens: Pulls the next page from the queue.
Hands off to the workflow controller.
nodes.why_matters: Predictable, parallel-safe execution.
nodes.example: Audit of 6 pages becomes 6 queued jobs.

nodes.label: Workflow Controller
nodes.purpose: The conductor — runs each audit step in the right order.
what_happens: Walks through browse → analyze → score → store.
Saves progress at every step.
Pauses for human review when needed.
nodes.why_matters: Audits run reliably end-to-end and survive crashes.
nodes.example: If the AI step fails, the controller retries without re-loading the browser.

nodes.label: Retry & Recovery
nodes.purpose: Catches failures and tries again sensibly.
what_happens: Detects timeouts, crashes, transient errors.
Reruns failed steps with backoff.
nodes.why_matters: One bad page never sinks the whole audit.
nodes.example: A 504 from a slow product page is retried twice before being skipped.

nodes.label: Browser Agent
nodes.purpose: Opens and controls a real browser to visit a page.
what_happens: Launches Chromium under Playwright.
Loads the URL with safe defaults.
nodes.why_matters: Audits use a real browser, not a stripped-down crawler.
nodes.example: Loads /checkout exactly as a real customer would.

nodes.label: Cookie & Overlay Handler
nodes.purpose: Closes pop-ups, banners, and chat widgets.
what_happens: Detects cookie consent, modals, chat widgets.
Dismisses them politely.
nodes.why_matters: Audits are not corrupted by overlays.
nodes.example: Cookie banner closed before the screenshot is taken.

nodes.label: Screenshot Capture
nodes.purpose: Takes pictures of the page.
what_happens: Captures full-page and viewport screenshots.
nodes.why_matters: Visual evidence for every finding.
nodes.example: Screenshot of the product detail page on mobile.

nodes.label: DOM & Accessibility Tree
nodes.purpose: Reads the page structure the way a screen reader would.
what_happens: Pulls the DOM and the accessibility tree.
Filters out ad and decoration noise.
nodes.why_matters: Findings are anchored to real on-page elements.
nodes.example: Builds a clean tree of headings, buttons, forms.

nodes.label: Multi-Viewport Capture
nodes.purpose: Repeats capture at desktop, tablet, and mobile sizes.
what_happens: Resizes the viewport.
Re-runs screenshot and DOM capture.
nodes.why_matters: Mobile-only conversion blockers don't slip through.
nodes.example: Catches a CTA hidden under a mobile sticky banner.

nodes.label: Initial Page State
nodes.purpose: Captures the page as it first loads.
what_happens: First-paint snapshot of structure and visuals.
nodes.why_matters: Baseline for comparing later states.
nodes.example: Stores the home-page hero before any interaction.

nodes.label: Scroll & Lazy-load States
nodes.purpose: Scrolls the page so lazy content appears.
what_happens: Scrolls to bottom in steps.
Triggers lazy-loaded sections.
Captures each segment.
nodes.why_matters: Content hidden until you scroll is still audited.
nodes.example: Catches a testimonial section that loads only after scroll.

nodes.label: Interaction States
nodes.purpose: Opens hidden views — modals, accordions, filters.
what_happens: Clicks safe interactive elements.
Captures the resulting state.
nodes.why_matters: Issues hidden behind clicks are surfaced.
nodes.example: Opens the size-filter panel on a product list and audits it.

nodes.label: State Coordinator
nodes.purpose: Decides which states are worth analyzing.
what_happens: Scores each state for conversion impact.
Selects a smart subset.
nodes.why_matters: Avoids wasting AI cost on low-value states.
nodes.example: Skips the empty 'help' modal; analyzes the filter panel.

nodes.label: Screenshot Library
nodes.purpose: Stores every screenshot per state and per viewport.
what_happens: Saves images and links to findings.
nodes.why_matters: Reports can show real evidence, not assertions.
nodes.example: Mobile screenshot of the broken checkout button.

nodes.label: DOM Evidence
nodes.purpose: Stores the structural snippet behind each finding.
what_happens: Records the relevant DOM and AX-tree fragment.
nodes.why_matters: Engineers can trace findings to actual code.
nodes.example: Captures the button HTML for a low-contrast CTA finding.

nodes.label: Annotated Visuals
nodes.purpose: Draws boxes and labels on screenshots.
what_happens: Marks the offending element on the screenshot.
Adds short captions.
nodes.why_matters: Clients see exactly what's wrong at a glance.
nodes.example: A red box around a hard-to-find search input.

nodes.label: Reproducibility Snapshot
nodes.purpose: Pins exactly how the audit was run.
what_happens: Records model name, prompt hash, heuristic versions, browser version.
nodes.why_matters: We can re-run any audit and explain the result.
nodes.example: Audit #4321 ran with claude-sonnet-4-2026-03 and heuristics v1.4.

nodes.label: CRO Heuristic Library
nodes.purpose: The 30 expert CRO rules.
what_happens: Each rule defines one common conversion problem and how to spot it.
Rules are private IP, never shared with clients.
nodes.why_matters: The team's expertise shows up in every audit, not just the senior ones.
nodes.example: Rule HEU-09: 'Primary CTA must be visible without scrolling on mobile'.

nodes.label: Business & Page Rules
nodes.purpose: Maps rules to business and page types.
what_happens: Tells the filter which rules apply where.
nodes.why_matters: B2B SaaS pages don't get e-commerce rules.
nodes.example: 'Add-to-cart trust badges' rule only on product detail pages.

nodes.label: Persona & Funnel Rules
nodes.purpose: Weights rules by persona and funnel stage.
what_happens: Boosts trust rules at checkout, awareness rules at top of funnel, etc.
nodes.why_matters: Findings are prioritized for the right buyer at the right stage.
nodes.example: Trust signals weighted higher on checkout for first-time buyers.

nodes.label: Two-Stage Filter
nodes.purpose: Picks only the rules that matter for this page.
what_happens: Stage 1: rule-based pre-filter on page type and persona.
Stage 2: similarity match against rule descriptions.
nodes.why_matters: Cuts AI cost and noise.
nodes.example: From 30 rules → 8 relevant rules for this product page.

nodes.label: Page Understanding
nodes.purpose: AI re-reads the page through the buyer's eyes.
what_happens: Combines page model + screenshots + persona.
Builds a higher-level understanding.
nodes.why_matters: Findings start from a buyer's lens, not a developer's.
nodes.example: Notes that the hero copy is jargon-heavy for a first-time visitor.

nodes.label: Heuristic Evaluation
nodes.purpose: Applies each shortlisted rule to the page.
what_happens: For each rule, the AI checks the page.
Returns pass / warn / fail with rationale.
nodes.why_matters: Surfaces the issues a senior consultant would flag.
nodes.example: Rule HEU-09 fails: CTA below the fold on mobile.

nodes.label: Self-Critique
nodes.purpose: AI challenges its own draft findings.
what_happens: A second AI persona reviews each draft.
Drops weak or hallucinated findings.
nodes.why_matters: Higher signal-to-noise. Fewer false positives.
nodes.example: Drops a 'low contrast' claim that was actually fine.

nodes.label: Fact-Check (Grounding)
nodes.purpose: Code-level verification of every finding.
what_happens: Runs deterministic checks against the live page.
Drops findings that don't pass.
10+ grounding rules in MVP.
nodes.why_matters: Findings are anchored to facts, not AI opinion.
nodes.example: Confirms the CTA's contrast ratio is actually 2.1, below the 4.5 threshold.

nodes.label: Evidence Annotation
nodes.purpose: Attaches evidence to each finding.
what_happens: Pulls the right screenshot region.
Pulls the DOM snippet.
Bundles them with the finding.
nodes.why_matters: Every recommendation comes with a paper trail.
nodes.example: Finding bundled with annotated mobile screenshot + button HTML.

nodes.label: Cross-Page Patterns
nodes.purpose: Finds issues that repeat site-wide.
what_happens: Groups similar findings across pages.
Promotes them to site-wide patterns.
nodes.why_matters: Clients hear about systemic issues, not one-off bugs.
nodes.example: All product pages have weak trust badges → site-wide pattern.

nodes.label: AI-Driven Discovery
nodes.purpose: Catches issues the rule library doesn't yet cover.
what_happens: AI re-reads the page free of any rule shortlist.
Surfaces findings from its general CRO and UX knowledge.
Tags every finding as 'novel' so the consultant reviews them carefully.
outputs: Novel draft findings (tagged for human attention)
nodes.why_matters: The system is not boxed in by what the rule library already knows. Consultant-approved novels become future heuristics — the library grows with use.
nodes.example: AI flags awkward checkout microcopy that no existing rule describes. Consultant approves it. The Heuristic Improvement Service later promotes it into a new rule.

nodes.label: CRO Issues
nodes.purpose: Final list of validated issues from the audit.
what_happens: Aggregates per-page and cross-page findings.
Removes duplicates.
nodes.why_matters: Single source for everything wrong with the site.
nodes.example: 12 issues across 6 pages.

nodes.label: Hypothesis Pipeline
nodes.purpose: Frames each issue as a testable hypothesis.
what_happens: Drafts 'if we change X, then Y will improve' statements.
nodes.why_matters: Audits become A/B-test-ready, not just observation lists.
nodes.example: 'If we move the trust badges above the CTA, conversion will improve on mobile.'

nodes.label: Prioritized Recommendations
nodes.purpose: Sorts hypotheses by impact and effort.
what_happens: Applies 4D scoring + impact and effort matrices.
Outputs a sequenced action plan.
nodes.why_matters: Clients leave with a roadmap, not a wishlist.
nodes.example: Top 3 quick wins this sprint, 2 medium-effort items next.

nodes.label: Executive Summary
nodes.purpose: 1-page summary for the CEO or marketing lead.
what_happens: Picks the top findings.
Writes a short narrative.
nodes.why_matters: Stakeholders grasp the audit in 60 seconds.
nodes.example: 'Mobile conversion is leaking at checkout. Three fixes recommended.'

nodes.label: Review Prep (Warm-up)
nodes.purpose: Gets the audit ready for consultant review.
what_happens: Groups findings by page and theme.
Flags low-confidence findings for closer attention.
nodes.why_matters: Consultants spend time on judgement, not triage.
nodes.example: Highlights a low-confidence trust-badge finding for human eye.

nodes.label: Consultant Approval
nodes.purpose: The hard pause before any audit ships.
what_happens: Audit is presented in the dashboard.
Consultant approves, edits, or rejects findings.
nodes.why_matters: Every audit is human-approved. Brand-safe.
nodes.example: Consultant removes one false positive, adjusts severity, approves.

nodes.label: Manual Override
nodes.purpose: Lets the consultant edit findings or add new ones.
what_happens: Edits finding text, severity, evidence.
Adds custom findings.
nodes.why_matters: Human expertise is preserved in the deliverable.
nodes.example: Consultant adds a brand-context finding the AI missed.

nodes.label: Stakeholder 1-Pager
nodes.purpose: The 1-page version for executives.
what_happens: Renders the executive summary in a friendly format.
nodes.why_matters: C-level reads in 60 seconds.
nodes.example: Sent to the CMO before the kickoff meeting.

nodes.label: Consultant Report
nodes.purpose: The full report with findings, evidence, and recommendations.
what_happens: Renders all findings, screenshots, scores, and the action plan.
nodes.why_matters: The deliverable consultants present in client meetings.
nodes.example: 30-page report with annotated screenshots per finding.

nodes.label: PDF Output
nodes.purpose: Generates a branded, share-ready PDF.
what_happens: Renders HTML and exports to PDF.
Stores PDF in object storage.
nodes.why_matters: Polished, offline-ready deliverable.
nodes.example: Acme-CRO-Audit-2026-04.pdf, 18 MB.

nodes.label: Dashboard View
nodes.purpose: Web view of the audit for the consultant team.
what_happens: Lists audits, findings, evidence.
Allows export and re-share.
nodes.why_matters: Central operating surface for the team.
nodes.example: Consultant filters all audits by client and exports.

nodes.label: Audit Database
nodes.purpose: Stores all audits, findings, scores, and links.
what_happens: Writes records using Postgres.
Per-client data is isolated.
nodes.why_matters: No audit is ever lost.
nodes.example: Query: 'all checkout findings across our last 50 audits'.

nodes.label: Object Storage
nodes.purpose: Stores screenshots, PDFs, and raw artifacts.
what_happens: Saves files with audit-scoped keys.
Returns signed URLs.
nodes.why_matters: Cheaper, scalable storage for large blobs.
nodes.example: All audit screenshots saved in R2 under /audits/4321/.

nodes.label: LLM Cost Log
nodes.purpose: Append-only record of every AI call.
what_happens: Logs prompt hash, tokens, cost, model, latency.
Never updated or deleted.
nodes.why_matters: Per-client billing transparency and budget enforcement.
nodes.example: Audit #4321 cost $11.40, 96% inside budget.

nodes.label: Activity Logs
nodes.purpose: Structured logs for every audit step.
what_happens: Writes JSON logs with audit ID and step.
Streams to a log aggregator.
nodes.why_matters: Bugs are diagnosed in minutes.
nodes.example: Find every audit that timed out on step 'fact-check'.

nodes.label: Progress Events
nodes.purpose: Live event stream for the dashboard.
what_happens: Emits start, page-done, audit-done events.
nodes.why_matters: Consultants see live progress, not a spinner.
nodes.example: Dashboard shows 4 of 6 pages complete.

nodes.label: Audit Outcomes Archive
nodes.purpose: Stores approved audits for later study.
what_happens: Approved audits are tagged and archived.
Outcomes link to consultant edits made during review.
AI-discovered findings outside the rule library are flagged 'novel' for follow-up.
nodes.why_matters: Provides the raw data the learning service needs — including the novel findings that will become tomorrow's rules.
nodes.example: Audit #4321 archived with note: 'consultant approved 1 novel finding on checkout microcopy'.

nodes.label: Edit & Feedback Patterns
nodes.purpose: Tracks how consultants change AI findings over time.
what_happens: Captures every edit and override during review.
Aggregates patterns across audits.
nodes.why_matters: Tells us which heuristics under-fire or over-fire in the real world.
nodes.example: 'Mobile CTA visibility' rule was edited up in 18% of audits → suggest threshold tightening.

nodes.label: Heuristic Improvement Service
nodes.purpose: Proposes heuristic updates and brand-new rules based on real audits.
what_happens: Reads the outcome archive and edit patterns.
Promotes consultant-approved novel findings into draft new rules.
Suggests edits to existing rules where edit-patterns show systematic drift.
Versioned, approved changes flow back into the rule library.
nodes.why_matters: The product gets smarter with use, not stale. Today's AI-discovered finding becomes tomorrow's heuristic.
nodes.example: After 50 audits, suggests tightening the 'mobile CTA visibility' threshold AND adds a new rule for 'checkout microcopy clarity' from approved novel findings.

nodes.label: Phased Roadmap
nodes.purpose: How Neural will be built — 10 phases, 263 tasks.
what_happens: Each phase has spec, plan, tasks, impact, and exit criteria.
Phases gate on each other so quality stays high.
nodes.why_matters: Predictable, gated delivery.
nodes.example: Phase 7 covers AI analysis. Phase 9 covers reporting. Each ships independently.

nodes.label: Spec Kit Harness
nodes.purpose: Keeps specs and code in sync as the product evolves.
what_happens: /speckit.* commands regenerate spec, plan, and tasks from the PRD.
Every artifact is lifecycle-tagged (draft → validated → approved → implemented).
nodes.why_matters: Specs never rot away from the codebase.
nodes.example: When the PRD changes, /speckit.tasks regenerates the task list automatically.

nodes.label: Deferred Features
nodes.purpose: What's coming after MVP.
what_happens: Features are listed in PRD non-goals.
Pulled into v1.1+ planning when ready.
nodes.why_matters: Disciplined scope today, clear path tomorrow.
nodes.example: v1.1: stealth browser + full learning loop · v1.2: GPT-4o fallback · v1.3: multi-tenant.
